# Feature: WebSocket Service

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-004  
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_websocket_sse.md`

Implement a server-side `WebSocketService` class in `packages/shared/src/services/` that manages the full lifecycle of WebSocket connections from within the Node.js runtime (VS Code extension host or `packages/server`). The service opens connections with custom headers, query params, auth (API key, Bearer, Basic), and TLS/proxy settings; routes incoming messages and status transitions to event-driven handler callbacks; lets callers send text messages; and closes connections cleanly. It is the shared backend primitive that both the VS Code adapter (FEAT-008) and the Web server adapter (FEAT-009) will delegate to.

**Business value**: Without this service, neither adapter can establish WebSocket connections. All WS UI features in FEAT-006, FEAT-008, and FEAT-009 are blocked on this service being in place.

---

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No persistent entities — all WebSocket state is ephemeral and lives in the Zustand store (FEAT-002). This feature adds only runtime types and a service class.

### New Types in `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts`

These types are placed in core so they can be referenced by the `IRealtimeAdapter` interface (added in FEAT-008) while also being imported by the shared service.

- **`Unsubscribe`** *(type alias)* — `() => void` — function returned by each `on*` callback registration; calling it removes the listener.

- **`WsConnectionConfig`** *(interface)* — parameters passed into `WebSocketService.connect()`:
  - `id` (`string`, NOT NULL) — caller-assigned unique connection ID (typically `crypto.randomUUID()` at the adapter layer)
  - `url` (`string`, NOT NULL) — fully-qualified WebSocket URL (`ws://` or `wss://`)
  - `headers` (`Record<string, string>`, optional) — extra upgrade-request headers
  - `params` (`string`, optional) — pre-serialised query string appended to the URL
  - `auth` (`Auth`, optional) — auth configuration; service resolves to headers using the existing `AuthServiceFactory`

- **`WsConnectionHandle`** *(interface)* — the event-driven handle returned by `WebSocketService.connect()`:
  - `connectionId` (`string`) — matches `WsConnectionConfig.id`
  - `onMessage(cb: (msg: WsMessage) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires for every incoming text frame
  - `onStatusChange(cb: (status: ConnectionStatus) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires on each lifecycle transition
  - `onError(cb: (error: string) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires on connection/stream errors
  - `onHeaders(cb: (headers: Record<string, string>) => void): Unsubscribe` — fires once with upgrade-response headers after a successful handshake

### Internal Type in `packages/shared/src/services/WebSocketService.ts`

- **`ActiveWsConnection`** *(internal interface, not exported)* — one entry per open connection kept in the service's private `Map<string, ActiveWsConnection>`:
  - `ws` — the `ws.WebSocket` instance
  - `config` — the original `WsConnectionConfig`
  - `messageListeners` — `Set<(msg: WsMessage) => void>`
  - `statusListeners` — `Set<(status: ConnectionStatus) => void>`
  - `errorListeners` — `Set<(error: string) => void>`
  - `headerListeners` — `Set<(headers: Record<string, string>) => void>`

### Relationships

- `WebSocketService` 1:N `ActiveWsConnection` — one service instance manages many connections; each connection is keyed by `connectionId`.
- `WsConnectionHandle` references `ActiveWsConnection` — the handle's `on*` methods add/remove listeners from the internal sets.

### Constraints / Limits

- Maximum 10 concurrent active connections enforced at `connect()` time (returns `null` with an error log when exceeded).
- URL scheme must be `ws:` or `wss:` — all other schemes are rejected before any network I/O.

---

## 4. Dependencies

| Feature | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| FEAT-001 — Protocol Types & Core Data Model | Provides `WsCollectionRequest`, `RequestProtocol`, type guards |
| FEAT-002 — Realtime State Slice | Provides `ConnectionStatus`, `WsMessage`, `RealtimeTabState` already in `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts`; this feature extends that file |
| `AuthServiceFactory` in `packages/shared` | `setAuthServiceFactory` pattern is already set up for `HttpService`; `WebSocketService` follows the same approach for auth header resolution |
| Node.js `crypto` (built-in) | Used for `crypto.randomUUID()` internally where needed |

> FEAT-008 (VS Code Adapter) and FEAT-009 (Web Adapter) depend on this feature — they cannot be started before FEAT-004 is complete.  
> `IRealtimeAdapter` and its addition to `IPlatformAdapter` are **deferred to FEAT-008** to avoid immediately breaking the existing `vsCodeAdapter` and `webAdapter` implementations.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add WS connection types to `@wave-client/core`

- [ ] **Implementation**:  
  Extend `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts`:
  - After the existing `RealtimeStateByTabId` type, add:
    1. `Unsubscribe` type alias: `export type Unsubscribe = () => void;`
    2. `WsConnectionConfig` interface with fields: `id`, `url`, `headers?`, `params?`, `auth?` (use the existing `Auth` union type from `./auth`).
    3. `WsConnectionHandle` interface with fields: `connectionId`, `onMessage`, `onStatusChange`, `onError`, `onHeaders` — each `on*` receives a typed callback and returns `Unsubscribe`.
  
  Extend `packages/core/src/index.ts`:
  - Add `Unsubscribe`, `WsConnectionConfig`, `WsConnectionHandle` to the types re-exported from `./types/realtime`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**:  
  No runtime logic introduced — TypeScript compilation is the verification gate. If the types are consumed in TASK-002's tests without cast errors, they are correct.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**:  
  Add JSDoc comments to each new type/interface in `realtime.ts`:
  - `Unsubscribe`: describe the cleanup contract.
  - `WsConnectionConfig`: describe each field (url scheme requirement, how params are appended, auth resolution).
  - `WsConnectionHandle`: describe the event-driven model; note that calling `connect()` schedules the connection asynchronously and initial status (`idle` → `connecting`) fires via `onStatusChange` before any data arrives.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build` exits with code 0; `Unsubscribe`, `WsConnectionConfig`, and `WsConnectionHandle` appear in `packages/core/dist/index.d.ts`.

### TASK-002: Implement `WebSocketService` — connection lifecycle (connect + receive + disconnect)

- [ ] **Implementation**:  
  Add `ws ^8` to `dependencies` and `@types/ws ^8` to `devDependencies` in `packages/shared/package.json`. Run `pnpm install` from the repo root to lock the new entry.

  Create `packages/shared/src/services/WebSocketService.ts`:

  ```
  Responsibilities:
  - Private `_connections: Map<string, ActiveWsConnection>` — keyed by connectionId.
  - MAX_CONNECTIONS = 10 module-level constant.
  - connect(config: WsConnectionConfig): WsConnectionHandle | null
      1. Validate URL scheme — must be ws: or wss:. Log warning + return null otherwise with error log.
      2. Check connection limit. Log warning + return null if at max.
      3. Build upgrade request headers:
         - Start with config.headers (if any).
         - If config.auth is set, resolve auth headers via _resolveAuthHeaders() (see below).
      4. Append config.params to URL query string if provided.
      5. Emit status 'connecting' via listeners (if any registered — none yet since handle is not
         returned until this call returns, but listeners may be added before the async open event fires).
      6. Create ws.WebSocket(url, { headers, agent }).
         - For wss:// URLs, retrieve the HTTPS agent via `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl(url)`.
         - Pass the agent (or undefined) to the ws constructor.
      7. Wire ws event handlers:
         - 'open': capture upgrade response headers from ws.response (cast if needed),
                   emit 'connected' status, emit headers.
         - 'message': parse text payload, create WsMessage (id=crypto.randomUUID(),
                      direction='received', size=Buffer.byteLength(data,'utf8'),
                      timestamp=Date.now()), emit to messageListeners.
         - 'close': emit 'disconnected' status; remove from _connections.
         - 'error': stringify error, emit to errorListeners, emit 'error' status,
                    remove from _connections.
      8. Store ActiveWsConnection in _connections.
      9. Build and return a WsConnectionHandle (closure over connectionId):
         - onMessage(cb): adds cb to messageListeners, returns () => delete cb.
         - onStatusChange(cb): adds cb to statusListeners, immediately calls cb with
                               current status if already connected/error, returns remover.
         - onError(cb): adds cb to errorListeners, returns remover.
         - onHeaders(cb): adds cb to headerListeners, returns remover.

  - disconnect(connectionId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>
      1. Look up connection in _connections. Return err if not found.
      2. Emit 'disconnecting' status.
      3. Call ws.close(1000, 'client disconnect').
      4. Return ok(). (The actual 'close' event will emit 'disconnected' asynchronously.)

  - sendMessage(connectionId: string, message: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>
      --- Implemented in TASK-003 ---

  - private _resolveAuthHeaders(auth: Auth, headers: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string>
      --- Implemented in TASK-003 ---

  - private _emitStatus(connectionId: string, status: ConnectionStatus): void
      Retrieves statusListeners for connectionId; calls each with status.

  - private _removeConnection(connectionId: string): void
      Deletes from _connections; logs lifecycle end.
  ```

  Export a module-level singleton: `export const webSocketService = new WebSocketService();`

  Update `packages/shared/src/services/index.ts`:
  - Add `export { WebSocketService, webSocketService } from './WebSocketService';`
  - Export `WsConnectionConfig` and `WsConnectionHandle` from `@wave-client/core` re-exports (if not already included via `@wave-client/core` re-exports in `packages/shared/src/types.ts`).  

  Update `packages/shared/src/types.ts`:
  - Re-export `Unsubscribe`, `WsConnectionConfig`, `WsConnectionHandle` from `@wave-client/core`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/shared/src/test/services/WebSocketService.test.ts`):

  Set up a `ws` mock using `vi.mock('ws', ...)` that returns a controllable fake `WebSocket` class:
  ```
  class MockWebSocket extends EventEmitter {
    static instances: MockWebSocket[] = [];
    readyState = 0; // CONNECTING
    send = vi.fn();
    close = vi.fn();
    constructor(url: string, opts?: unknown) { super(); MockWebSocket.instances.push(this); }
    simulateOpen(headers = {}) { this.readyState = 1; this.response = { headers }; this.emit('open'); }
    simulateMessage(data: string) { this.emit('message', data); }
    simulateClose(code = 1000) { this.readyState = 3; this.emit('close', code, ''); }
    simulateError(err: Error) { this.emit('error', err); }
  }
  ```

  Also mock `storeService` (same pattern as HttpService tests).

  **Tests to write:**

  | ID | Scenario | Assertion |
  |----|----------|-----------|
  | T01 | Valid `ws://` URL — connect succeeds | `ws.WebSocket` constructed with correct URL; `onStatusChange` fires `connecting` |
  | T02 | Valid `wss://` URL — HTTPS agent requested | `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl` called with URL; agent passed to ws constructor |
  | T03 | `onStatusChange` fires `connected` on ws `open` event | stub `simulateOpen()`; verify `connected` callback |
  | T04 | `onHeaders` fires with upgrade headers on `open` | stub open with `{ 'x-server': 'test' }`; verify header callback |
  | T05 | `onMessage` fires with correct `WsMessage` on `message` event | stub `simulateMessage('hello')`; verify direction=`received`, content=`hello`, size=5 |
  | T06 | `onError` fires + status becomes `error` on ws `error` event | stub `simulateError(new Error('conn refused'))`; verify error string and status |
  | T07 | `onStatusChange` fires `disconnected` on ws `close` event | stub `simulateClose()`; verify `disconnected` status |
  | T08 | `disconnect()` emits `disconnecting` and calls `ws.close()` | call disconnect; verify `disconnecting` via onStatusChange; verify `ws.close` called |
  | T09 | `disconnect()` returns `err` for unknown connectionId | verify Result.isOk === false with descriptive message |
  | T10 | Invalid scheme `http://` — `connect()` returns null | no ws constructed; error logged |
  | T11 | Invalid scheme `javascript:` — `connect()` returns null | no ws constructed |
  | T12 | Connection limit — 11th connect returns null | open 10 connections first; 11th returns null |
  | T13 | `Unsubscribe` removes listener — no further callbacks | register `onMessage`, call unsubscribe, fire message, verify cb not called |
  | T14 | `onStatusChange` with already-`connected` state fires immediately | connect + simulateOpen; then add new onStatusChange; verify immediate call with `connected` |
  | T15 | Closed connection removed from map — duplicate disconnect returns err | connect, simulateClose, call disconnect again → err result |

- [ ] **Documentation Update**:  
  Add JSDoc to `WebSocketService` class, `connect()`, `disconnect()`, `MAX_CONNECTIONS`, and the `ActiveWsConnection` internal type. Document the `ws` dependency reason in `packages/shared/README.md`.

- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared test` passes all new tests and all pre-existing shared tests. `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared build` exits 0.

### TASK-003: WebSocketService — `sendMessage` + auth/proxy header integration

- [ ] **Implementation**:  
  In `packages/shared/src/services/WebSocketService.ts`:

  **`sendMessage(connectionId, message)`**:
  1. Look up connection; return `err('Connection not found: ...')` if missing.
  2. Check `ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN`. Return `err('Not connected: ...')` otherwise.
  3. Create a `WsMessage` record (direction=`'sent'`, content=message, size=`Buffer.byteLength(message, 'utf8')`, id=`crypto.randomUUID()`, timestamp=`Date.now()`).
  4. Emit the message to `messageListeners` so the UI can show it in the timeline immediately.
  5. Call `ws.send(message)` in a `try/catch`; on error, return `err(String(e))`.
  6. Return `ok(void)`.

  **`_resolveAuthHeaders(auth: Auth, baseHeaders: Record<string, string>): Promise<Record<string, string>>`**:
  - Follows the same pattern as `HttpService.executeRequest()`: calls `authServiceFactory?.getService(auth.type)?.applyAuth(...)`.
  - Only handles `Bearer`/`ApiKey`/`Basic` — Digest and NTLM auth emit a `console.warn` "Auth type X is not supported for WebSocket connections" and are skipped.
  - If `authServiceFactory` is not set, returns `baseHeaders` unchanged.
  - This method is called asynchronously in `connect()` before the ws constructor (update TASK-002's `connect()` to be `async` and `await` this step, or resolve auth before creating the ws).

  Update `connect()` to be `async` and call `_resolveAuthHeaders` before constructing the ws instance. The return type becomes `Promise<WsConnectionHandle | null>`. Update the JSDoc accordingly.

  > **DEVIATION NOTE**: The plan's `IRealtimeAdapter.connectWebSocket()` signature is synchronous (returns `WsConnectionHandle`). Making `connect()` async changes the internal service API. The adapter implementation (FEAT-008) can resolve this by eagerly resolving auth before calling the service, or by accepting a Promise-returning handle. This deviation must be noted in FEAT-008's spec for resolution. The `WsConnectionHandle` itself remains synchronous (callbacks, not promises).

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (append to `WebSocketService.test.ts`):

  | ID | Scenario | Assertion |
  |----|----------|-----------|
  | T16 | `sendMessage` — connected → ws.send called + message emitted to listeners | verify `ws.send('hello')` called; verify `onMessage` fires with direction=`'sent'` |
  | T17 | `sendMessage` — websocket not OPEN → err result | set readyState to CLOSING; verify `Result.isOk === false` |
  | T18 | `sendMessage` — unknown connectionId → err result | verify `Result.isOk === false`, message mentions the id |
  | T19 | `sendMessage` — ws.send throws → err result | `ws.send.mockImplementation(() => { throw new Error('broken pipe') })`; verify err |
  | T20 | Auth: ApiKey auth adds header to upgrade request | mock authServiceFactory returning header `{ Authorization: 'Bearer tok' }`; connect; verify ws constructed with `{ headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer tok' } }` |
  | T21 | Auth: Digest auth is skipped with warning | mock auth type `digest`; connect; verify no auth header in ws opts; verify `console.warn` called |
  | T22 | Auth: no authServiceFactory set → headers unchanged | connect without calling `setWsAuthServiceFactory`; no error thrown |
  | T23 | `connect()` is async — awaited handle is fully functional | `await webSocketService.connect(config)` returns a handle; then `simulateOpen()` fires `connected` |

- [ ] **Documentation Update**:  
  Update JSDoc for `connect()` to reflect async signature and the auth-resolution step. Document the deviation note. Add `sendMessage` JSDoc describing the sent-message echo to the timeline.

- **Verify**: All 23 tests (T01–T23) pass. `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared build` exits 0. `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build` exits 0.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: Bearer, API key, and Basic auth are resolved to `Authorization` / key headers that are sent with the WebSocket upgrade request. This mirrors `HttpService`. Digest and NTLM are not applicable and are silently skipped with a `console.warn`. Auth secrets are never logged.
- **Error Handling**: `connect()` returns `null` (not `Result`) for pre-flight validation failures (invalid URL, limit exceeded) because no handle exists yet — callers must null-check. `sendMessage()` and `disconnect()` return `Result<void, string>`. Runtime connection errors (network drop, server close) are surfaced via the `onError` and `onStatusChange` callbacks on the handle.
- **Logging**: Log at `console.info` level: connection attempts (`[WebSocketService] connect attempt id=<id> url=<url>`), successful open (`[WebSocketService] connected id=<id>`), close (`[WebSocketService] disconnected id=<id> code=<code>`), and errors (`[WebSocketService] error id=<id> msg=<message>`). Log URL without query string in production logs to avoid leaking tokens in params. Never log auth headers or tokens.
- **Security**: URL scheme validation is enforced synchronously before any auth resolution or network I/O. Connection limits prevent resource exhaustion. TLS certificate settings are applied via the existing `storeService.getHttpsAgentForUrl()` agent.
- **Performance**: Listener sets use native `Set`; `_emitStatus` iterates the set synchronously on the Node.js event loop (no async). This is fine for the expected listener count per connection (≤ 3 UI components).

---
